tool.name: EnzyWizard Dock
tool.description: Perform molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure

param.name: max_docking_attempt_num
param.label: Maximum docking attempts
param.help: Maximum number of docking attempts. Default: 20

param.name: no_early_stop
param.label: Disable early stop
param.help: Disable stopping immediately after the first successful docking result.

param.name: exhaustiveness
param.label: Exhaustiveness
param.help: Exhaustiveness of AutoDock Vina search. Default: 16

param.name: cpu
param.label: Number of CPUs
param.help: Number of CPUs used by AutoDock Vina. Default: 0 (auto)

param.name: min_rad
param.label: Minimum probe radius
param.help: Minimum probe radius used in pocket detection. Default: 1.8

param.name: max_rad
param.label: Maximum probe radius
param.help: Maximum probe radius used in pocket detection. Default: 6.2

param.name: catalytic_residue
param.label: Catalytic residue index
param.help: Cleaned protein residue index used as the docking box center.

param.name: catalytic_site_coord
param.label: Catalytic site coordinate
param.help: Catalytic site center coordinate separated by ','. Example: 12.5,8.0,-3.2

param.name: box_size
param.label: Box size
param.help: Docking box size. Required when catalytic_residue or catalytic_site_coord is provided.

tool.help:
**EnzyWizard-Dock**
Performs molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure using AutoDock Vina. Supports both single-substrate and multi-substrate docking with multiple conformations.
**Inputs** - Cleaned protein structure in CIF or PDB format. - Substrate names separated by ';'. - Directory containing substrate SDF files.
**Outputs** - JSON report with docking results.
For more information, see the `enzywizard-dock` documentation: https://github.com/bioinfbrad/enzywizard-dock
